$comment_1: Halvren Cycle Diary — public log of desk actions on the 20-operator coverage universe.
$comment_2: Newest first. Drives /diary, /diary/<id>, /diary/feed.xml, and the homepage 'Latest from the desk' block.
$comment_3: Action values: promoted, demoted, flagged, added, killed, reviewed.

entries.summary: Q1 cash flow exceeded guide by 18% and the buyback pace doubled.
Mexico royalty proposal advanced in legislature; thesis sensitivity revisited.
FY 2025 print and 26th consecutive raise reread against the succession question.
Q1 AISC printed inside guide and the producer-of-the-decade case held.
Westinghouse contribution disclosed at investor day; integration ahead of plan.
Public writeup refreshed against the FY 2025 release and the buyback pace.
Mainline apportionment normalised; the turnpike read remains intact.
Attachie Phase 1 in service; the condensate strip moved per-share value the right way.
53rd consecutive raise telegraphed in the annual; the rate-base compounding still works.
Foster Creek turnaround scope widened; full-year capex creeping above plan.
Free cash flow trajectory below sustaining capex through cycle on the revised price deck.
Potash netbacks back inside the historical band; retail margin still doing the work.
South Bow spin-off operating metrics diverging; rereading the remainco maturity ladder.
Operating ratio held inside 58–60% range; the franchise is still earning the multiple.
Buffett stake disclosure unchanged at 28%; Berkshire signalling, not buying.
Refining utilization at 97% and unit cost down for a fourth quarter running.
QB2 ramp curve still flattening; revised through-cycle ROIC walks closer to cost of capital.
Cedar LNG sanctioned with the strip locked; long-dated take-or-pay book extended.
Permian gas backlog conversion accelerating; coverage 2.3× on the distribution.
Indonesian smelter delay added six months to the export licence overhang.
BC mill curtailment offset by the U.S. South capacity additions; through-cycle FCF intact.
Detour Lake throughput at a new high; the producer-of-the-decade case keeps holding.
Term book signings ran double the 2024 pace at a higher floor price.
Dominion utility rate cases closed inside the requested band; the take-or-pay base reads cleanly.
Post-South-Bow remainco capex pulled back to the regulated base; balance sheet looks lighter.
